LAW AND MOTION 302, ATTORNEY MICHAEL S. BURKE'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND PG&E. RULING: IN CAMERA HEARING HELD AS ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE MIGHT BE DISCLOSED. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL. THE COURT RECOMMENDS THAT THE MATTER BE REFERRED TO THE PRESIDING JUDGE FOR TRIAL CONTINUANCE AS IT WOULD BE PREJUDICIAL FOR CCSF & PG&E TO BE WITHOUT COUNSEL. THE TRANSCRIPT OF THIS PROCEEDING IS UNDER SEAL AND NOT TO BE RELEASED EXCEPT ON ORDER OF THE COURT. MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER. (302/REQ). HON. RONALD E. QUIDACHAY. Notce Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Nov-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ATTORNEY MICHAEL S. BURKE'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND PG&E.
APPEARANCES: Nicole Meredith, Esq., 415-398-0200 of Vogle & Meredith for CCSF and PG&E Defendant and Moving Party; John P. Aubrey, Esq., 415-554-3800 for CCSF Opposing Party; Barbara J. Damlos, Esq., 415-973-7136 for Defendant PG&E; Bryan J. McCormackm, Esq., 415-440-6662 for Plaintiff.
RULING: In camera hearing held as attorney/client privilege might be disclosed. The Court grants the motion to be relieved as counsel. The Court recommends that the matter be referred to the Presiding Judge for trial continuance as it would be prejudicial for CCSF & PG&E to be without counsel. The transcript of this proceeding is under seal and not to be released except on order of the court. Moving party to submit proposed order. (302/REQ). Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR #2647
